what_it_does: An acceptance test that exercises the backend cognition VIEW: it proves that Suite.cognition_info() projects the live cognition registries via build_cognition_info, that cognition_capabilities() reuses the same role serializer, that extra role files appear dynamically in the projection, that malformed registry entries fail loudly, that cognition.* event kinds are narration-only and documented in runtime/AGENTS.md, and that a staged turn emits cognition.* lifecycle events satisfying causal invariants (with a loud skip when the resident 4B at :8000 is unavailable).
observations: The module-level docstring maps each numbered test section to design-doc clauses (06-rendering.md, C9.2, C9.4, R2-FOLD H5).
It uses a custom check() helper and a global PASS counter instead of a unittest/pytest harness.
It constructs a temporary FsStore, NodeRegistry, RoleRegistry, and Suite for isolated acceptance runs.
It compares suite.cognition_info() against live registries for roles, edge_kinds, thought_shapes, activation_contexts, rule_ops, destination_kinds, casts, and event_kinds.
It verifies role and rule serialization fields expected by the frontend cognition-node card and rule badge.
It asserts that cognition_capabilities() roles are a strict subset of cognition_info() roles, proving a shared serializer.
It copies the real focus role into a temporary extra_roles directory and adds a new mynewrole.py to demonstrate dynamic projection without code changes.
It tests fail-loud behavior: a role key/id mismatch raises in _serialize_role, and malformed thought_shapes or activation_contexts raise in build_cognition_info.
It checks the C9.2 floor: every COGNITION_EVENT_KINDS key starts with cognition. and contains no resolve/approve/dispatch, and each declares an address field.
It checks the C9.4 drift home: every event kind, build_cognition_info, and /api/cognition_info appears in runtime/AGENTS.md.
The by-use section probes http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1/models and loudly skips if the resident 4B is down.

When the resident is up, it stages a listening-mode turn, filters store events by cognition.* and turn_id, sorts by seq, and asserts causal invariants: turn.start first, turn.done last, all role.fire before all role.ran, final part after role.ran, inject before final part with source and run://turn/source address, every event has a top-level address, and the cognition.wave rollup still emits.
standouts: The test is organized as numbered acceptance sections that trace directly to design-document clauses.
It proves dynamic, registry-driven projection by injecting a new role file into a temporary directory and observing it in build_cognition_info.
It asserts causal invariants of concurrent cognition lifecycle events rather than enforcing a flat deterministic order.
conventions: Uses module-level imperative code with a try/finally cleanup block rather than a test framework.
Uses noqa: E402 comments to allow imports after sys.path manipulation.
Uses tempfile and shutil for isolated fixture directories.
Uses a docstring as the primary specification and traceability document.
Uses urllib.request for a simple service health check.
Breaks typical unit-test isolation by requiring an external resident model service for the full lifecycle proof.
concerns: Global mutable PASS counter and check() side effect make the test stateful and harder to compose.
Module-level sys.path.insert and os.chdir mutate global interpreter state.
Hardcoded resident endpoint URL and timeout.
Reading runtime/AGENTS.md from disk couples the test to documentation file contents that can drift independently.
The ad-hoc _FakeRole class only partially implements the role interface.
If the resident 4B is unavailable, the by-use section is skipped, reducing coverage without failing.
No explicit test runner harness; an assertion failure aborts the entire script.
list(suite2.chat_parts(...)) may block indefinitely if streaming does not terminate.
notes: A design-traceable acceptance test that treats live registries, documentation, and concurrent cognition lifecycle events as first-class proof targets.
questions: What is the exact schema returned by build_cognition_info and Suite.cognition_info()?
How are seq and turn_id assigned to store events?
What is the resident 4B and how is it expected to be started before running this test?
What are the precise semantics of role fields such as mode_scope, can_fire, is_jury, draws, and requires?
How does the runtime guarantee the causal invariants under actual concurrent role execution?
Is runtime/AGENTS.md updated automatically, or is the drift-home check a manual gate?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the docstring matches the executed checks: projection, reuse, dynamic discovery, fail-loud validation, narration floor, drift home, and causal lifecycle invariants.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the new cognition VIEW backend auditable and trustworthy: it wants to show that frontend rendering data is always derived from live registries, that serializer logic is not duplicated, that registries can grow without code changes, that bad data raises rather than silently corrupts, that cognition events remain narration-only, that runtime documentation stays in sync, and that the concurrent cognition lifecycle has observable causal structure.
novelty: It is an acceptance test that uses design-document section numbers as its structure, proves registry-driven projection by file injection, and verifies concurrent event causal invariants instead of a deterministic sequence.
contribution: A runnable acceptance contract that demonstrates the cognition projection endpoint, role-serializer reuse, dynamic role discovery, validation defenses, event-kind floor, documentation drift check, and causal lifecycle invariants for the cognition VIEW backend.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test for the cognition VIEW backend that validates registry-driven projection, serializer reuse, dynamic role discovery, fail-loud validation, event-kind constraints, documentation drift, and causal lifecycle event invariants.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that the backend cognition VIEW is observable, registry-true, dynamically extensible, fail-loud, narration-only, documentation-aligned, and causally well-ordered under concurrency.

symbols.does: A test helper that asserts a condition and increments a global PASS counter.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) prints an ok message when cond is true and increments PASS; otherwise it raises an AssertionError with the given label, acting as the file's minimal assertion primitive.

symbols.does: A helper that returns True if a callable raises an exception.
symbols.description: _raises(fn) executes fn inside a try/except and returns True if any exception is raised, used to assert fail-loud behavior without needing to name the exact exception type.

symbols.does: Builds a new Suite instance over a temporary store and the project's nodes directory.
symbols.description: fresh_suite(tmp) creates an FsStore in tmp/store, discovers nodes from NODES, and returns a Suite plus the store, providing an isolated fixture for each acceptance section.

endpoint_up() attempts a short HTTP GET to the resident model endpoint and returns True only if the response status is 200; used to decide whether the by-use lifecycle section can run or must be skipped.

symbols.does: A minimal stand-in class used to test role key/id validation.
symbols.description: class _FakeRole exposes id, spec, can_fire, is_jury, draws, mode_scope, and requires so the test can verify that _serialize_role raises when the registry key disagrees with role.id.

symbols.does: Finds the first or last index of a given event kind in the chronological event list.
symbols.description: idx(kind, last=False) scans the sorted cognition.* event list and returns the first or last position of kind, returning None if the kind is absent; it supports the causal-invariant assertions in the by-use section.
